exams.title: LLD Final Exam
exams.description: Comprehensive exam covering SOLID principles and structural patterns

questions.question: A class that handles user creation, email sending, and report generation violates which SOLID principle?
questions.explanation: This class has multiple reasons to change (user logic, email logic, report logic), violating SRP.

questions.question: Which pattern lets you treat a tree of objects the same way as a single object?
questions.explanation: Composite pattern allows uniform treatment of individual objects and compositions.

questions.question: The Adapter pattern changes the behavior of the adapted class.
questions.explanation: Adapter only converts the interface; it does not change the underlying behavior.

questions.question: Which design approach best follows the Open/Closed Principle?
options: Adding elif branches for new types
Using a Strategy pattern with new implementations
questions.explanation: Strategy pattern allows extension through new strategy classes without modifying existing code.

questions.question: Which patterns are classified as Structural?
questions.explanation: Adapter and Facade are structural patterns. Observer and Command are behavioral.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of the Flyweight pattern?
questions.explanation: Flyweight reduces memory by sharing common (intrinsic) state across many objects.

questions.question: In the LLD 6-step framework, which step takes the most time?
questions.explanation: Implementation takes 15-20 minutes out of a 45-minute interview.

questions.question: A Virtual Proxy creates the expensive object immediately on construction.
questions.explanation: A Virtual Proxy defers creation of the expensive object until it's actually needed (lazy loading).

questions.question: Which principle is violated if a Bird base class has a fly() method but Penguin extends Bird?
questions.explanation: Penguin can't fly, so it can't fulfill the Bird contract — a Liskov Substitution Principle violation.

questions.question: Which UML relationship means 'part cannot exist without the whole'?
questions.explanation: Composition implies strong ownership where the part's lifecycle is tied to the whole.

exams.title: HLD Final Exam
exams.description: Comprehensive exam covering distributed systems fundamentals and networking

questions.question: A system that chooses Availability over Consistency during a network partition is called:
questions.explanation: AP systems prioritize availability, accepting eventual consistency during partitions.

questions.question: Which load balancing algorithm sends each request to the server with the fewest active connections?
questions.explanation: Least Connections routes to the server handling the fewest active connections.

questions.question: gRPC uses JSON as its default serialization format.
questions.explanation: gRPC uses Protocol Buffers (protobuf), a binary serialization format, not JSON.

questions.question: How many QPS (queries per second) is roughly 1 million requests per day?
Which are advantages of horizontal scaling over vertical scaling?
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling provides redundancy and theoretically unlimited scale, but adds architectural complexity.

questions.question: What is the first step in the DERSC framework?
questions.explanation: Always start by defining and clarifying functional and non-functional requirements.

questions.question: Which real-time protocol provides server-to-client streaming only (not bidirectional)?
questions.explanation: SSE is unidirectional: server pushes updates to the client over a persistent HTTP connection.

questions.question: A CDN can serve both static files and dynamic API responses.
questions.explanation: Modern CDNs can cache dynamic responses with appropriate cache headers, though they're primarily used for static content.

questions.question: 99.9% availability allows how much downtime per year?
questions.explanation: 99.9% (3 nines) = 0.1% downtime = 365.25 * 24 * 0.001 = 8.76 hours per year.

questions.question: Which component sits between clients and backend services, handling auth, rate limiting, and routing?
questions.explanation: API Gateway is the single entry point handling cross-cutting concerns like auth, rate limiting, and routing.

exams.title: Full System Design Mock Exam
exams.description: Combined LLD + HLD exam simulating real interview conditions

questions.question: Which SOLID principle does the Strategy pattern primarily leverage?
questions.explanation: Strategy pattern allows extending behavior through new strategies without modifying existing code — the essence of OCP.

questions.question: For a URL shortener handling 100M URLs, which storage is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: At 100M scale, a sharded relational DB (or NoSQL) provides durability, indexing, and horizontal scaling.

questions.question: In a parking lot LLD problem, the Parking Lot class should contain the logic for payment calculation.
questions.explanation: Payment logic should be separated (SRP). ParkingLot manages spots; PaymentService handles billing.

questions.question: Which pattern is best for implementing undo/redo functionality?
questions.explanation: Command pattern encapsulates operations as objects with execute() and undo() methods, enabling undo/redo.

questions.question: Twitter's news feed uses which approach for celebrity users with millions of followers?
questions.explanation: Twitter uses a hybrid: push for normal users, pull for celebrities to avoid massive write amplification.

questions.question: Which are valid caching strategies?
questions.explanation: Cache-aside, write-through, and write-behind are standard caching strategies.

questions.question: In an elevator system LLD, which pattern manages elevator states (IDLE, MOVING_UP, MOVING_DOWN)?
questions.explanation: State pattern encapsulates state-specific behavior, perfect for elevator state transitions.

questions.question: Consistent hashing solves the problem of redistributing all keys when a node is added or removed.
questions.explanation: Consistent hashing minimizes key redistribution — only K/N keys need to move when a node changes.

questions.question: Which approach prevents double-booking in a hotel reservation system?
questions.explanation: Optimistic locking (version check on update) prevents double-booking while maintaining concurrency.

questions.question: What is the main trade-off of eventual consistency?
questions.explanation: Eventually consistent systems may return stale data briefly, trading immediate consistency for higher availability.
